div.p: he further remarked, there could be no evil in it, if God gave His sanction by healing; that there could be no more sin in any female laying hands on and praying for the sick, than in wetting the face with water; it is no sin for anybody to administer that has faith,1 or if the sick have faith to be healed2 by their administration.

He reproved those that were disposed to find fault3 with the management of the concerns of the Church, saying God had called him to lead the Church, and he would lead it right; those that undertake to interfere will be ashamed when their own folly is made manifest;4 that he calculates to organize the Church in its proper order as soon as the Temple is completed.

President Smith continued by speaking of the difficulties he had to surmount ever since the commencement of the work, in consequence of aspiring men.5 “Great big Elders,” as he called them, who caused him much trouble; to whom he had taught the things of the kingdom in private councils, they would then go forth into the world and proclaim the things he had taught them, as their own revelations; said the same aspiring disposition will be in this Society, and must be guarded against; that every person should stand, and act in the place appointed,6 and thus sanctify the Society and get it pure. He said he had been trampled under foot7 by aspiring Elders, for all were infected with that spirit; for instance, John E. Page and others had been aspiring;8 they could not be exalted, but must run away as though the care and authority of the Church were vested with them. He said he had a subtle devil9 to deal with, and could only curb him by being humble.
div.div: The Prophet’s Intimation of His Death
div.p: As he had this opportunity, he was going to instruct the ladies of this Society, and point out the way10 for them to conduct themselves,11 that they might act according to the will of God;12 that he did not know that he should have many opportunities of teaching them, as they were going to be left to themselves;13 they would not long have him to instruct them; that the Church would not have his instructions long, and the world would not be troubled with him a great while, and would not have his teachings [in person].
